title: Beyond Good and Evil
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
content: “He who fights with monsters should look to it that he himself does not become a monster. And if you gaze long into an abyss, the abyss also gazes into you.”
action: Ensure your methods against an adversary don't corrupt your own standards.

title: The Origin of Species
author: Charles Darwin
content: “It is not the strongest of the species that survives, nor the most intelligent that survives. It is the one that is most adaptable to change.”
action: Prioritize agility and adaptability over static power to ensure system survival.

title: Leviathan
author: Thomas Hobbes
content: “No arts; no letters; no society; and which is worst of all, continual fear, and danger of violent death; and the life of man, solitary, poor, nasty, brutish, and short.”
action: Maintain standardized infrastructure and protocols to avoid systemic collapse.
